And he told them, ‘I really wanted to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
Because I say to you; I won’t eat it again until it’s fulfilled in the Kingdom of God.’
Then, accepting a cup, he gave thanks and said, ‘Take this and pass it among yourselves.
Because I tell you; From now on I definitely won’t drink from the product of the vine again until the Kingdom of God has arrived.’
Then he took a loaf [of bread], gave thanks, broke it, and gave it to them saying, ‘This is my body, which is being handed over for you. Keep doing this in memory of me.’
And he did the same thing with the cup after supper, saying, ‘This cup is the New Sacred Agreement of my blood, which is being poured out on your behalf.
But look… the hand of my betrayer is with me here at the table!
Because the Son (who is for a fact from men) is going the way that was marked out for him… yet woe to that man through whom he’s betrayed!’
So, they started trying to figure out among themselves which of them would really be the one who was going to do this.
However, there was also an argument that broke out among them over which one seemed to be the greatest.
So he said to them: ‘Kings of the nations act like masters, And the powerful wish to be called benefactors.
But you should not be that way. The greatest should act like the youngest, And the one taking the lead as the servant.
‘For, which is truly the greatest… He who reclines at the table, Or the one who does the serving? Isn’t it he who reclines at the table? Yet, I am the one who is acting As the servant among you.
‘You’re those who stayed with me during my trials,
So I’m making this promise to you, As my Father made a promise to me, for a Kingdom:
In my Kingdom, you’ll eat and drink at my table And upon thrones you will sit, To judge Israel’s twelve tribes.
‘Simon, O Simon; Look! The Opposer has demanded to have you men, so [he can] sift you like wheat.
But I’ve pleaded with [God] on your behalf, that your faith doesn’t give out, and that when you return you will strengthen your brothers.’
Then [Peter] said to him, ‘Lord, I’m ready to go with you to prison and to death!’
But he replied, ‘I tell you this Peter; A rooster won’t crow today until you’ve denied knowing me three times.’
Then he told them, ‘When I sent you out without a money bag, food pouch, or sandals, you didn’t need anything, did you?’ And they replied, ‘No!’
Then he said, ‘But now; Whoever has a moneybag must carry it along, as well as a pouch for his food. And those who don’t have a sword, should sell his cape and then buy one.
I tell you this: What was written about me must now be fulfilled… And he was numbered among the criminals. So the thing that has been bothering me so much, is now about to be finished.’
Then they said, ‘Look, Lord; Here are two swords!’ And He said, ‘That’s enough.’
Well, he then left [the city], and went to the Mount of Olives as usual; and the disciples followed along.
And when he got there, he told them, ‘Now, pray that you won’t be tempted.’
Then he walked away from them about a stones throw, bent down and started to pray,
saying, ‘Father; If you wish, let this cup pass from me. However, may Your will not mine, take place.’
Then he saw a messenger from heaven that strengthened him.
Yet he was still in agony, so he prayed even harder; and his sweat became like drops of blood falling on the ground.
Then, after the prayer, he got up and returned to the disciples and found them sleeping because of their grief.
So he asked, ‘Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray that you aren’t tempted!’
But while he was still talking, {Look!} there was a mob coming, and one of the twelve (called Judas) was leading them. Then he came up to Jesus to kiss him.
But Jesus asked, ‘Judas; Do you betray the Son of Man with a kiss?’
And when those who were around him saw what was about to happen, they asked, ‘Lord, shall we fight them off with the sword?’
And one of them struck the Chief Priest’s slave, taking off his right ear.
Then Jesus said, ‘It has gone far enough;’ and he touched the ear and healed him.
Then Jesus asked the Chief Priests, Temple captains, and elders who came for him, ‘Are you coming with swords and clubs [as though I were] a robber?
Why, I was with you in the Temple every day, and you didn’t try to grab me then… but this is your hour and the power of darkness.’
So they arrested him and led him off, taking him to the house of the High Priest, as Peter was following at a distance.
Then they lit a fire in the middle of the courtyard and they all sat down together; and Peter sat down among them.
That’s when a servant girl noticed him sitting in the light of the fire. She looked at him hard, and said, ‘He was also with him!’
But [Peter] denied it, saying, ‘I don’t know him, woman!’
And a little while later, someone else saw him and said, ‘You’re one of them too!’ But Peter said, ‘Man, I’m not!’
Then, after standing around for another hour, someone started insising, saying, ‘It’s a fact; This one was with him, because he’s a Galilean too!’
But Peter said, ‘Man, I don’t know what you’re talking about!’ And just as he was speaking, a rooster crowed.
Then the Lord turned and looked at Peter, and Peter remembered the words that the Lord said to him, ‘Before a rooster crows today, you will deny knowing me three times.’
And he went outside and cried bitterly.
Now, the men who had him in custody started making fun of him, and they whipped him.
Then they covered [his head] and said, ‘Prophesy… who’s disciplining you?’
And they kept saying all sorts of slanderous things about him.
Well, as it became day, the people’s body of elders assembled with the Chief Priests and scribes, and they brought him before the Jewish High Court, saying,
‘If you’re the Anointed One, tell us!’ But he answered, ‘If I told you, you wouldn’t even start to believe it.
And if I asked you any questions, you wouldn’t answer at all.
But from now on, the Son of Man will be sitting at the powerful right hand of God!’
Well at this, they all asked, ‘So, are you the Son of God?’ And he said, ‘You are saying that I am!’
And they said, ‘Why do we need further witnesses? We’ve heard it all from his own mouth!’
So, the whole assembly got up and took [Jesus] to Pilate.
And there they accused him, saying, ‘We found this man trying to undermine the principles of our nation by forbidding us to pay taxes to Caesar, and by saying that he’s the Anointed One… a king!’
So Pilate asked him, ‘Are you the king of the Jews?’ And he replied, ‘You’re the one who is saying this.’
Then Pilate told the Chief Priests and crowds, ‘I can’t find anything wrong with this man.’
But they insisted, saying, ‘He has been stirring up the people by teaching throughout all of Judea… [and that’s the reason why] he came here from Galilee!’
On hearing that, Pilate asked whether the man was a Galilean.
And after learning that he was from Herod’s realm, he sent him on to Herod (who was also in Jerusalem [for the Festival]).
Well, when Herod saw Jesus, he was overjoyed, because he had been wanting to see him for a long time, since he had heard so much about [Jesus], and he hoped to see him perform some sign.
So he asked many questions, but [Jesus] wouldn’t answer.
[And all the while], the Chief Priests and scribes kept getting up and accusing him.
But, not knowing what to make of him, Herod and his troops made fun of him by dressing him in bright clothes, then they sent him back to Pilate.
And it was because of this that Herod and Pilate became friends that day, since they really didn’t like each other before that.
So, Pilate then assembled the Chief Priests, the rulers, and the people,

and told them: ‘You brought this man to me as someone who was inciting the people to revolt. And look, I’ve examined him in front of you and I can’t find any basis for the charges you’re bringing against him.
In fact, Herod didn’t either, because he returned him to us. Look! He’s done nothing deserving of death,
so I’ll discipline him and release him.’
But with that, the entire crowd started shouting, ‘Hang him up! Release BarAbbas to us!’
([BarAbbas] was in prison because of [his involvement in] a revolt in the city and for murder.)
Again Pilate called out to them, because he wanted to release Jesus.
But they started yelling, ‘Impale him! Impale him!’
So he asked them a third time: ‘Why… what bad thing did he do? I haven’t found anything he’s done that deserves death, so I’ll discipline him and let him go!’
Then they started shouting impatiently, demanding to have him hung on a pole, and their voices began to win out.
So Pilate decided to give in,
and he released [BarAbbas] (who was in prison for rebellion and murder) to meet their demands, but did what they wanted [him to do] to Jesus.
Now, as they were taking him away, they grabbed Simon (a Cyrenian who was coming in from the fields) and laid the impaling pole on him, so that he could carry it behind Jesus.
Well, a huge crowd of people, including many women, followed behind, beating themselves in grief and wailing over him.
Then Jesus turned to the women and said, ‘Daughters of Jerusalem; Stop crying for me… cry for yourselves and for your children!
Because, {Look!} the days are coming when people will say: Those without any children; Those whose wombs didn’t give birth; And whose breasts didn’t nurse are the blest ones!
‘Then they’ll say to the mountains; Fall upon us! And to the hills, Come cover us over!
For. if they do these things when the tree is still moist, What will happen after it’s withered?’
Two criminals were also being led out to be impaled with him,
and when they got to the place that was referred to as ‘The Skull,’ they hung him and the criminals on poles there (one on his right and the other on his left).
However, Jesus was saying: ‘Father; Forgive them, for they don’t know what they are doing,’ as they were throwing dice to divide his clothes [among them].
And the people just stood there watching. But their rulers were angrily looking on and saying, ‘He saved others, so let him save himself if he’s the Anointed One of God, the Messiah!’
Even the soldiers made fun of him, coming up and offering him soured wine
and saying, ‘If you’re the king of the Jews, save yourself!’
There was also an inscription above him [that said], ‘This is the king of the Jews.’
Then, one of the criminals who was hanging there started to speak badly to him saying, ‘Aren’t you the Anointed One? Save yourself… and us!’
But the other scolded him saying, ‘Aren’t you at all afraid of God, since you’ve received the same judgment?
And that’s only right, because we’re getting what we deserve in full for the bad things we’ve done. But he didn’t do anything wrong!’
Then he said, ‘Jesus; Remember me when you come into your Kingdom.’
And [Jesus] replied, ‘I tell you this today; You will be with me in Paradise.’
Well, it was already about the sixth hour, and the whole land was covered by a darkness [that lasted] until the ninth hour,
because the sunlight had failed. Then the Temple curtain split down the middle,
and Jesus called in a loud voice, ‘Father, I leave my breath in Your hands.’ And after saying that, he died.
And when the centurion saw what had happened, he glorified God and said, ‘This truly was a righteous man.’
Well, all the crowds who came to watch this spectacle (when they saw the things that had happened) started beating themselves [in grief] again.
And meanwhile, all who knew him, and the women who had followed him from Galilee, were standing at a distance and watching these things.
Then {Look!} a man named Joseph (from AriMathea, a city in Judea), who was a good and righteous man, and one of the Council members
who didn’t go along with what they said or did, and who was also awaiting the Kingdom of God,
went to Pilate and asked for Jesus’ body.
So he took it down, wrapped it in fine linen, and laid it in a tomb that no one had been laid in before, which was carved from rock.
Well, it was the day of Preparation, and the evening light was fading into the Sabbath.
So the women who had come from Galilee with him, followed along to look at the tomb and to see how his body was laid out.
Then they went back to prepare spices and perfumed ointments… but of course, they followed the Commandments and rested on the Sabbath.
It was on the first day of the week, at early dawn, that they went to the tomb carrying the spices they had prepared.
But they found the stone rolled away from the tomb,
and when they entered, they couldn’t find the Lord Jesus’ body.
And as they stood there confused, {Look!} two men in glowing clothes appeared beside them.
This frightened them and they hung their heads to the ground. Then [the men] asked, ‘Why are you looking for the living among the dead?
He isn’t here; he got up! Don’t you remember? When he spoke to you in Galilee,
he said; The Son of Man must be betrayed into the hands of sinful men and be hung on a pole, then [he will be] resurrected on the third day.’
So, that’s when they remembered what he said,
and they left the tomb to report all these things to the eleven and the others.
It was Mary of Magdala, Joanna, Mary the [mother] of James, and the rest of the women who told the Apostles these things.
However, what they said sounded like nonsense, and they didn’t believe them.
Then Peter got up and ran to the tomb. And when he leaned in, all he saw was the wrappings. So he left, wondering just what had happened.
{Look!} That very same day, two of them were on their way to a village named Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem,
and they were discussing all the things that had happened.
Now, as they were talking and reasoning, Jesus himself came up and started walking along with them,
but their eyes refused to recognize him.
So he asked, ‘What is the subject you’re debating as you’re walking along?’ Then they stopped and looked very sad.
And the one named Cleopas answered, ‘Are you living alone in Jerusalem so that you don’t know what has happened over the past few days?’
And [Jesus] asked, ‘What things?’ They replied, ‘The things [that happened to] Jesus the Nazarene… a Prophet who came with powerful words and deeds before God and all the people.
Then, how our Chief Priests and rulers gave him over to be sentenced to death, and hung him on a pole.
But we were hoping that he was the one who was going to free Israel… it has been three days since these things happened!
‘Then, some women among us shocked us. They went to the tomb early [this morning]
and couldn’t find his body. So they came back saying that they had a vision of messengers [from God] who say he’s still alive.
Then, some from among us went to the tomb and found it just as the women said… they didn’t see him.’
So [Jesus] said to them, ‘O senseless ones whose hearts are slow when it comes to believing all the things that the Prophets said;
Wasn’t it necessary for the Anointed One to suffer these things and then enter into his glory?’
So, starting from Moses and the Prophets, he explained all the scriptures that talked about him.
Well, when they reached the village that they were traveling to, he acted as though he was going on.
But they pressured him, saying, ‘Stay with us, because it’s getting late and most of the day is already gone!’ So, he stayed with them.
And as he reclined with them at the meal, he took the loaf, spoke a blessing over it, broke it, and started handing it to them.
And at that, their eyes were fully opened and they recognized him… then he disappeared.
And they said to each other, ‘Weren’t our hearts burning as he talked to us along the road, completely explaining the Scriptures to us?’
So they got up right away and returned to Jerusalem. There they found the eleven with the rest who had gathered,
who told them: ‘The Lord was in fact raised, and he appeared to Simon!’
Then they also told what had happened [to them] on the road and how he became recognizable by breaking the loaf.
Well, while they were still talking about these things, there he stood in their midst saying, ‘May you have peace.’
But this frightened and terrified them, because they thought they were seeing a [wicked] spirit.
So he said to them, ‘Why are you so worried, and what kind of ideas are your hearts coming up with?
See… my hands and my feet. It’s me! Touch me and look, because a spirit doesn’t have flesh and bones as you see that I have!’
And as he said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.
But because they still didn’t believe (due to the joy and confusion), he asked, ‘Do you have something to eat?’
So they handed him a piece of broiled fish,
and he took it and ate it in front of them.

Then he said to them, ‘These are the things that I told you while I was still with you, for everything that was written about me in the Law of Moses, in the Prophets, and in the Psalms had to be fulfilled.’
Then he opened their minds to understand the meanings of the Scriptures completely.
And he said to them, ‘It was written that the Anointed One would suffer this way and rise from the dead on the third day.
Then, [the message of] repentance for forgiveness of sins would be preached to all the nations in his name, starting from Jerusalem.
Now, you are to be the witnesses of these things.
‘And look; I’m sending you [something] that was promised by my Father. So, stay in the city until you are clothed with the power from above.’
And with that, he led them out on the road to BethAny, and then he lifted his hands and blest them.
And as he was blessing them, he was taken away from them into the sky.
Then they bowed low to him, and they returned to Jerusalem in joy.
[And after that], they were in the Temple continuously, praising God.
In the beginning there was the Word. The Word was with The God and the Word was a powerful one.
This one was with The God in the beginning,
and through him it all came to be… life came to be,
and this life was the light of all men.
[Now] the light is shining in darkness, and he hasn’t been defeated by the darkness.
Then came a man named John who had been sent by God.
He came as a witness to testify about the light, so that all might believe through him.
He wasn’t the light, but he was to testify about the light.
For, the true light that enlightens all men was coming into the world.
He was in the world – and through him the world came to be – but the world didn’t know him.
He came to his home, but his own people offered no welcome.
Yet, he gave all those who did welcome him (all those who believed in his name) the authority to become the children of God.
And they weren’t born from blood, from the will of the flesh, or from man’s will, but from God.
So the Word became flesh and tented among us; and we looked on his glory, which was the glory of the only generated one next to the Father, and he was filled with kindness and truth.
John witnessed about him. He’s the one who said it; he even yelled it out loud: ‘He who’s coming behind me came before me, because he was first… before I was!’
We all received kindness after kindness from his wealth.
And though the Law was given through Moses, kindness and truth came through Jesus the Anointed.
For, no one has ever seen God, but the only-generated god (the one in the favored position of the Father) has explained Him.
Now, this is the testimony that John gave when the Judeans sent Priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him who he was.
[John] confessed… he didn’t deny it, he confessed, ‘I’m not the Anointed One!’
And when they asked, ‘Then, who [are you]… are you EliJah?’ He said, ‘No, I’m not.’ ‘Are you the Prophet?’ And he answered, ‘No!’
Finally, they asked, ‘Then, just who are you, so we can give an answer to those who sent us… what do you have to say about yourself?’
And he said, ‘I’m the voice of someone calling out in the desert, Straighten the way of the Lord, as the Prophet IsaiJah said.’
Now, those who came were sent by the Pharisees,
and they asked him, ‘Then, why do you baptize if you aren’t the Anointed One, or EliJah, or the Prophet?’
And John replied, ‘I baptize in water, but there’s someone standing in your midst whom you don’t know.
He’s the one coming behind me, the laces of whose sandals I’m unworthy to untie.’
These things happened in BethAny across the Jordan where John was baptizing.
Well, the next day, he saw Jesus coming toward him and he said, ‘Look! The Lamb of God who lifts sin from the world!
This is the one I meant when I said, The one coming behind me has come before me, because he was first, before I was.
Even I didn’t know him, but the reason why I came baptizing in water was so that he might be shown to Israel.’
John also testified to this: ‘I saw the Breath [of God] coming down from the sky like a dove… and it stayed on him!
Even I didn’t know him, but the One who sent me to baptize in water told me, Whoever you see the Breath coming down on and remaining upon; this is the one who baptizes in Holy Breath…
I saw it and I testify to the fact that this is the Son of God!’
Then, the next day, John was standing there with two of his disciples.
And when he saw Jesus walking by, he again said, ‘Look, the Lamb of God!’
And the two disciples who heard him say this, followed Jesus.
Then Jesus turned, and noticing them following him, asked, ‘What are you looking for?’ And they replied, ‘Rabbi (Teacher), where are you staying?’
Then he said, ‘Come and see.’ So, they went and saw where he was staying and spent the day with him, until about the tenth hour.
Then Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter (one of the two who heard what John said and followed [Jesus])
went and found his brother Simon and told him, ‘We’ve found the Messiah (which translates as the Anointed One).’
And then he led him to Jesus. And when Jesus saw him, he said, ‘You’re Simon, the son of Jonah… you will be called Cephas (Peter).’
The next day, he wanted to leave for Galilee. But then Jesus found Philip and said to him, ‘Be my follower.’
Now, Philip was from BethSaida, the same city as Andrew and Peter.
Then Philip [went and] found NathaniEl (BarTholomew) and told him, ‘We’ve found the one who Moses wrote about in the Law and the Prophets, Jesus the son of Joseph from Nazareth!
But NathaniEl asked, ‘Can anything good come out of Nazareth?’ Then Philip said, ‘Well, come and see!’
And when Jesus saw NathaniEl coming, he said, ‘Look; An Israelite who really says what’s on his mind!’
Then NathaniEl asked, ‘How do you know me?’ And Jesus replied, ‘Before Philip called you, while you were under the fig tree, I saw you.’
Then NathaniEl said, ‘Rabbi; You’re the Son of God! You’re the King of Israel!’
And Jesus asked, ‘Do you believe this simply because I said that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than that.’
Then he said, ‘I tell you men the truth; You will see the heavens open up and messengers of God ascending and descending to the Son of Man!’
Now, on the third day there was a wedding banquet in Cana of Galilee, and Jesus’ mother was there,
so Jesus and his disciples were also invited to the banquet.
And when the wine started to run out, Jesus’ mother said to him, ‘They don’t have any more wine.’
But Jesus said, ‘Why is this any of my business, woman? My hour hasn’t come yet.’
Then his mother told the waiters, ‘Do whatever he tells you to do.’
Well, there were six stone water jars sitting there, which the Judeans required for washing, and each held fifteen gallons.
Then Jesus told them to fill the jars with water, and they were filled to the brim.
Then he told them, ‘Now dip some out and take it to the banquet director.’ So, they took it [to him],
and when the banquet director tasted the water that had been turned into wine (he didn’t know where it came from, but the waiters who drew the water knew), the director called the bridegroom
and said: ‘Everyone else puts out the fine wine first, and after people are intoxicated, the inferior. But you’ve saved the finest wine until now!’
So, there in Cana of Galilee was where Jesus started the signs that showed his glory, and his disciples put faith in him.
After that; he, his mother, his brothers, and [his] disciples went down to CapharNaoum, but they didn’t stay there long.
For, the Jewish Passover was near, so Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
And there he found [people] in the Temple selling cattle, sheep, and doves, as well as currency exchangers, who were sitting there.
So, after making a whip out of ropes, he drove all those with the sheep and cattle out of the Temple, and he poured out the currency exchangers’ coins, turning their tables over.
Then he told those who were selling the doves ‘Get these things out of here! Quit turning my Father’s House into a market!’
And his disciples remembered that it is written: ‘Zeal for Your House will consume me.’
Well after that, the Judeans asked, ‘Since you’re doing all these things, what sign can you show us?’
And Jesus answered, ‘Tear down this Temple and I’ll rebuild it in three days.’
Then the Judeans asked, ‘This Temple took forty-six years to build, and you’ll rebuild it in three days?’
Of course, he was talking about the Temple of his body.
However, when he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered that he used to say this, and they believed the Scriptures and the things that Jesus said.
It was while he was in Jerusalem for the Passover and its festival, that many people started believing in his name… after they saw the signs that he was performing.
But Jesus didn’t trust them, because he knew them…
he didn’t need anyone to tell him about people, because he knew what they were on the inside.
Now, there was a man named NicoDemos (one of the Pharisees and a ruler of the Judeans)
who came to him one night and said, ‘Rabbi; We know that you came as a teacher from God, because no one can perform signs like you’re doing unless God is with him.’
Then Jesus said, ‘I tell you the truth; Unless someone is born from above, he can’t see the Kingdom of God.’
So NicoDemos asked, ‘How can a man be born after he’s old… he can’t go back into his mother’s womb a second time to be born, can he?’
And Jesus replied, ‘I tell you the truth; Unless someone is born from water and [God’s] Breath, he can’t enter the Kingdom of God.
Because, things that have been born from flesh are fleshly, and things that have been born from the Breath (Spirit) are spiritual.
However, don’t be surprised because I told you that people have to be born again…

for the wind blows wherever it wishes, and you can hear the noise, but you don’t know where it’s coming from or where it’s going. And this is true of everyone who has been born from the Breath [of God].’
Then NicoDemos asked, ‘How can things like this happen?’
And Jesus answered, ‘You’re a teacher of Israel, and yet you don’t know?
I tell you the truth; We know what we’re saying and we’re testifying to things that we’ve seen; but you aren’t accepting our testimony…
and if you don’t believe the earthly things, how will you believe if I tell you heavenly things?
‘No one has gone to heaven other than he who came from heaven, the Son of Man.
And as Moses raised the snake in the desert, that’s how the Son of Man must be raised,
so that everyone who believes in him can get life in the age.
‘This is how [much] God loved the world: He gave His only-generated Son so that everyone who believes in him won’t be destroyed, but will get life in the age.
Why, God didn’t send his Son into the world to judge it, but for the world to be saved through him!
So, whoever believes in [the Son] won’t be condemned. However, those who don’t believe have already been condemned, because they haven’t shown faith in the name of God’s only-generated Son,
since this is [the basis for their] judgment. ‘The light came into the world, but men loved darkness more than light, because the things they were doing were wicked.
Those who are doing bad things hate the light, and they don’t come into the light so [they won’t] be disciplined for the things they are doing.
But those who are doing what is true come to the light, so the things that they are doing can be shown as having been done with God.’
Well, after that, Jesus and his disciples traveled into the Judean countryside where he spent some time with them, doing baptizing.
John was also baptizing in Aenon near Salim (there’s a lot of water there) and people kept coming to be baptized [by him],
because John hadn’t been thrown into prison yet.
Therefore, there was some discussion between John’s disciples and [a certain] Judean concerning purification.
So they came to John and said, ‘Rabbi; The man who was with you across the Jordan (who you testified about) is also baptizing, and everyone is going to him!’
Then John replied: ‘A man can have nothing unless he receives it from heaven.
And you are witnesses that I said I’m not the Anointed One, but I have been sent ahead of him.
The one with the bride is the bridegroom. But the bridegroom’s friend, when he stands and listens to him, gains much joy from the voice of the bridegroom. So, my joy is now complete,
and he must increase while I decrease.
‘He who’s from above is over all, but he who comes from the earth comes from the ground and talks about things that are earthly. He who comes from heaven is above all the others,
for he tells about things that he’s seen and heard. However, no one is accepting his testimony;
yet he who welcomes his testimony is proving God to be true.
And, since the one sent by God speaks God’s words, he doesn’t just dole out [small] portions of [God’s] Breath.
‘The Father loves the Son, and He has entrusted all things into his hands;
so he who believes in the Son will have life in the age; but those who disobey him won’t see life; for, the wrath of God stays on them.’
Now, when the Lord realized that the Pharisees had learned that [he] was making and baptizing more disciples than John
(although Jesus really didn’t really do any baptizing, his disciples did),
he left Judea and returned to Galilee.
And on the way, he had to travel through Samaria,
arriving at a Samaritan city called Sychar, which was close to the field that Jacob gave to his son Joseph,
and where Jacob’s [well] was located. Now, Jesus was tired from the journey, so he sat down at the edge of the [well] (at about the sixth hour),
and a Samaritan woman was there, who had come to draw water. So Jesus said to her, ‘Give me a drink.’
In the meantime, his disciples had gone into the city to buy food.
But the Samaritan woman asked him, ‘So, why are you, a Judean, asking me, a Samaritan woman, for a drink -- ?
And Jesus replied, ‘If you knew about the gift of God and who’s asking you for a drink, you might have asked him, and he would have given you living water.’
Then she said: ‘Master, you don’t have anything to draw water with… and it’s a deep well! So, where will you get this living water?
You aren’t greater than our ancestor Jacob who gave us this well and who drank from it with his sons and cattle, are you?’
Then Jesus replied, ‘Whoever drinks this water will just get thirsty again.
But whoever drinks the water that I give him won’t be thirsty again in this age, for the water that I give will become a fountain inside him that bubbles up life through the age.’
Then the woman said, ‘Lord, give me this water so I won’t be thirsty again and have to keep coming to this place to draw water!’
Then he said to her, ‘Go and call for your husband to come here.’
But the woman said, ‘I don’t have a husband.’ And Jesus said, ‘It’s good that you say you don’t have a husband,
because you’ve had five husbands; and the one you have now isn’t your husband… you’ve told the truth.’
And the woman said, ‘Lord, I see that you’re a Prophet.
Our ancestors worshiped here on this mountain, but you people say that Jerusalem is where people must worship.’
Then Jesus said to her, ‘Believe me, woman; the time is coming when you won’t worship the Father on this mountain or in Jerusalem!
And though you worship what you haven’t known, and we worship what we have known (because salvation comes from the Jews),
the time is coming (and it’s now) when true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and truth… because the Father is searching for people like this to worship Him.
For God is spirit, so those who worship Him must worship him in spirit and truth.’
Then the woman said, ‘I know that the Messiah (who is called the Anointed One) is coming, and when he gets here, he will explain it all to us.’
And Jesus said, ‘I (the one speaking to you) am he.’
Well, it was then that his disciples arrived and they were puzzled, because he was talking to a woman; and of course, no one asked what she was looking for or why he was talking to her.
Then the woman left her water jar, and went back into the city and told the men,
‘Come here and see a man who told me everything I did… this couldn’t be the Anointed One could it?’
So, they came to him from the city.
Meanwhile, the disciples were urging him, saying, ‘Rabbi, eat!’
But he told them, ‘I have food to eat that you’re unaware of.’
So the disciples started asking each other, ‘No one has brought him anything to eat, have they?’
Then Jesus said, ‘Doing the will of the One who sent me and finishing His work is my food.
Don’t you say that there are four more months until the harvest? {Look!} I tell you… look up and see the fields, for they’re white and ready for harvesting!
The harvester is already getting his pay and gathering in the fruit of life in this age; so the planter and the harvester can now rejoice together.
For the saying is true: ‘One does the planting, and another does the harvesting.’
I sent you out to harvest what you didn’t work on… so yes, others did the work, but you’re now benefiting from their efforts!’
Thereafter, many of the Samaritans from that city put faith in [Jesus] because of what the woman testified to when she said, ‘He told me everything I did.’
So when the Samaritans came to him, they asked him to stay with them, and he spent a couple of days there.
And as the result, many more believed because of the things he said.
Then they told the woman, ‘We don’t believe just because of what you said, but because we’ve heard for ourselves and we know that this man is truly the Savior of the world!’
Well, after two days, he left for Galilee,
and there, Jesus himself witnessed the fact that a Prophet has no honor in his hometown.
Yet, when he [first] arrived in Galilee, the Galileans had welcomed him, because they’d seen all the things he did in Jerusalem at the festival (since they’d been there too).
Then he returned to Cana of Galilee (where he turned the water into wine), and it was there that a man of royal blood whose son (who was in CapharNaoum at the time) was ill,
and when this man heard that Jesus had come out of Judea into Galilee, he went and asked him to heal his son who was about to die.
However, Jesus said to him, ‘You [people] just refuse to believe unless you see signs and omens.’
But the (royal) man urged him, saying, ‘Lord, come on, before my little boy dies!’
And finally Jesus said, ‘Go on your way! Your son is alive.’ So, the man believed what Jesus said and went away.
Then on his way back, his slaves met him and told him that his boy was alive.
And when he asked them exactly what time he got better, they told him, ‘The fever left him yesterday at the seventh hour.’
And the father recognized that it was the same time that Jesus told him, ‘Your son is alive.’ So he and his whole household started to believe.
This was the second sign that Jesus performed when he came out of Judea into Galilee.
Later on, there was a Jewish festival, so Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
And there in Jerusalem, at the Sheep-gate Pool (called BethZatha in Hebrew), are five stone columns
where many who were sick, blind, lame, and those with physical handicaps were all lying down.
And there was a man who had been extremely weak for thirty-eight years.
So when Jesus saw this man lying there (being aware of the long time he’d had this condition), Jesus asked him, ‘Do you want to get well?’
And the sick man answered, ‘Lord, I don’t have anyone to put me into the pool when the water is shaking. And while I’m trying to get in, someone else steps in ahead of me!’
Then Jesus said, ‘Get up, pick up your cot, and walk around.’
And with that, the man immediately became healthy, picked up his cot, and walked around. Now, that day was a Sabbath,
so the Judeans started telling the cured man, ‘It’s the Sabbath, so it’s illegal for you to carry the cot!’
But he told them, ‘The one who cured me said, Pick up your cot and walk!’
And they asked, ‘Which man was it that told you to pick it up and walk?’
But the healed man didn’t know who he was, because Jesus had faded into crowd.
Then later on, Jesus found the man in the Temple and told him, ‘See… now you’re healthy! Don’t sin anymore, so that something worse doesn’t happen to you.’
Well, the man went off and told the Judeans that it was Jesus who had cured him.
And this is why the Judeans started persecuting Jesus, because he was doing these things on the Sabbath.
But he told them, ‘My Father has kept working until now, and I keep working.’
As the result, the Judeans started looking [for ways to] kill him. Because, not only was he breaking the Sabbath, but he also called God his own Father, making himself equal to God.
Then Jesus said to them, ‘I tell you the truth; The Son can’t do anything on his own, other than what he sees the Father doing. Whatever [the Father] does is what the Son does.
And because the Father cares about the Son, He shows him everything that He does… and [God] will show him greater works than these, just to amaze you.
‘As the Father raises the dead and makes them alive, the Son can also make whoever he wishes alive.
And the Father doesn’t judge anyone, but He has left all the judging to the Son,
so that all may honor the Son as they honor the Father. And whoever doesn’t honor the Son doesn’t honor the Father who sent him.

‘I tell you the truth; He who hears what I say and believes in the One that sent me, will have ages of life… he won’t come into the judgment, for he has crossed over out of the death and into the life!’
‘I also truthfully say that the time is coming … when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God and those who listen will live.
For, just as the Father has life in Himself, He has also given the Son life in himself,
and authorized him to do judging. This is because he is the Son of Man.
‘Don’t be surprised at this, because the hour is coming when everyone in the tombs will hear his voice
and come out; those who did good things to a resurrection of life, and those who practiced what is bad to a resurrection of judgment.
‘I can’t do anything on my own; I judge by what I hear, and my judgments are righteous, because I’m not looking to do the things that I want, but to do the will of Him that sent me.’
‘If I should testify about myself, that testimony isn’t true.
But when someone else testifies about me, I know that the testimony he gives about me is true.
‘Now, you’ve sent [people] to John and he testified to the truth.
However, I don’t require the testimony of men, because I’m telling you these things to save you.
‘[John] was a burning, shining lamp and you wanted to rejoice in his light during his time.
However, I have a testimony that’s greater than John’s, because I’m doing the work that my Father assigned me to finish. It’s these things that testify about me [and prove] that the Father sent me.
‘Why, even the Father who sent me has testified about me. But you’ve never heard His voice or seen His shape,
and His words don’t stick with you, because you don’t believe the One whom He sent.
Yet, you keep searching the Scriptures, because you think they will give you life in the age; and though they testify about me,
you still refuse to come to me so you can have life!
‘[Because you] men don’t glorify me,
I know that you don’t have God’s love in you.
I’ve come in my Father’s Name, but you don’t welcome me; yet if someone else came in his own name, you would welcome him.
So, how can you believe when you receive glory from each other and you don’t look to glorify the one who comes from the only God?
‘But don’t think that I’m going to accuse you to the Father, for he who accuses you is Moses… the one in whom you’ve put your hope.
Because, if you believed Moses you would also believe me, for he wrote about me.
However, if you don’t even believe what he wrote, then how will you believe the things that I’m saying?’
After this, Jesus crossed to the other side of the Sea of Galilee (or Tiberius).
However, a large crowd kept following him just to see the signs he was performing upon those who were sick.
There, Jesus went up a mountain and sat down with his disciples.
And since it was close to the time of the Jewish Passover festival;
when Jesus looked up and noticed the huge crowd coming, he asked Philip, ‘Where should we buy bread for everyone to eat?’
However, he just said this to test him, because he already knew what he was going to do.
Then Philip answered, ‘Why, two hundred silver coins worth of loaves wouldn’t be enough to provide everyone with just a little [taste]!’
And one of his disciples (Andrew the brother of Simon Peter) said to him,
‘Here’s a little boy who has five loaves of barley bread and two small fish. But, what good is this when there are so many?’
Then Jesus said, ‘Have the people lie down as they would at a meal.’ Now, there was a lot of grass there, so the people (about five thousand of them) reclined.
Then Jesus took the bread, and after giving thanks, distributed it to those lying there. [He also did the same with] the small fish, [giving them as much] as they wanted.
But when they were all full, he told his disciples, ‘Now pick up the leftovers, so nothing is wasted.’
And when they picked them up, they filled twelve baskets with the leftovers from the five loaves of barley that those who ate had left behind.
So, when the men saw the signs he performed, they started saying, ‘This really is the Prophet who was to come into the world.’
And Jesus (because he knew that they were about to come and take him to make him their king) went away again, up on the mountain by himself.
That evening, his disciples went down to the sea,
got into a boat, and traveled to the other side of the Sea to CapharNaoum. Well, by then it had grown dark and Jesus hadn’t come to them yet.
And meanwhile, a strong wind came up and the seas became very rough.
Well, it was after they had been rowing for about three or four miles, that they saw Jesus walking on the sea approaching the boat, and this frightened them.
But he said to them, ‘Don’t be afraid… it is me!’
So they were finally willing to let him into the boat. And soon they came to shore at the place where they were going.

Then the next day, the crowd that was on the opposite side of the sea saw just the single little boat, but not the other, and they knew that Jesus hadn’t gotten into the boat with his disciples and that [the disciples] had left by themselves.
However, some boats arrived from Tiberias, close to the place where they had eaten the bread after the Lord gave thanks.
And when the crowd realized that Jesus and his disciples weren’t there, they got into the little boats and went to CapharNaoum to look for Jesus.
Then, when they found him across the sea, they asked, ‘Rabbi, when did you get here?’
And Jesus answered, ‘I tell you the truth; You aren’t looking for me because you saw signs, but because you had enough bread to eat.
Don’t work for food that can spoil. Rather, [work for] for the food of life that lasts through the age, which the Son of Man will give you, because he was sealed by the Father (The God).’
Then they asked, ‘What must we do to perform the works of The God?’
And Jesus answered, ‘This is what the work of The God is: Believing in the one whom He sent.’
So they asked, ‘Then, what sign will you perform for us so we can see and believe… what are your deeds?
Why, our ancestors ate the manna in the desert, for [the Scriptures tell us]: He gave them bread from heaven to eat.’
Then Jesus replied, ‘I tell you the truth; Moses didn’t really give you the bread from heaven, but my Father will give you the true bread from heaven.
For, God’s bread is [he] who comes from heaven and gives life to the world.
Then they said, ‘Lord, always give us this bread!’
And Jesus went on to say, ‘I’m the bread of life, and whoever comes to me won’t ever get hungry; and he who believes in me won’t ever get thirsty.
But I tell you this: You’ve seen me, yet you still don’t believe.
Everything that the Father gives me will come to me, and I won’t reject anyone who comes to me,
because I didn’t come from heaven to do my will, but [to do] the will of the One who sent me.
‘This is what the will of the One who sent me is: That I shouldn’t destroy anything He has given me out of [the world]; but rather, that I should resurrect it… [on] the Last Day.
Yes, this is what my Father’s will is: That everyone who pays close attention to the Son and believes in him should have life in the age, and I will resurrect him… [yes] me, on the Last Day!’
At this, the Judeans started grumbling about him, because he was saying that he’s the bread that came out of heaven.
So, they asked, ‘Isn’t this Jesus the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we all know? So, how is it that he’s telling us that; I have come down out of heaven?
Then Jesus replied, ‘Stop your grumbling!
No one can come to me unless the Father who sent me draws him [to me] and then I will resurrect him in the last day.
Why, it is written in the Prophets: They will all be those who are taught by God. So, everyone who listens when [they are] next to the Father and then learns, comes to me.
‘It’s not that anyone has seen the Father, other than the one who was beside The God… for, he has seen the Father.
I tell you the truth: He who believes has life in the age!
‘I am the bread of life.
Your fathers ate the manna in the desert and died,
but this is the bread that comes from heaven and no one who eats it will die.
I’m the living bread that came from heaven, and if anyone eats this bread, he will gain life in the age. The bread that I will give is my flesh, and it will provide life to the world.’
Well, at that, the Judeans started fighting with each other, asking, ‘How can this man give us his flesh to eat?’
So Jesus told them, ‘I tell you the truth; if you don’t eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you won’t live.
He who chews my flesh and drinks my blood will have life in [this] age, and then I will resurrect him on the Last Day,
because my flesh is truly food and my blood is truly drink.
He who chews on my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me, and I in him.
‘The living Father sent me and I’m living through the Father. Likewise, he who chews on me will live through me.
This is the bread: He who came down from heaven. It’s not the same as that which your fathers ate and then died, for he who chews on this bread will live into the age.’
Now, these are the things that he said while he was teaching in the synagogue at CapharNaoum.
Well, when they heard this, many of his disciples said, ‘Those are tough words. Who can listen to them?’
But Jesus, knowing what his disciples were grumbling about, asked them, ‘Is this a trap for you?
Then what will happen if you see the Son of Man going back up to where he came from?
[Your] breath is what makes you alive, for [without it] the flesh is useless. And these things that I’ve told you are life and breath,
yet some of you don’t believe.’ Jesus knew from the start who wouldn’t believe and who would betray him,
so he went on to say, ‘This is why I told you that no one can come to me unless it is given to him by the Father.’
But as the result of this, many of his disciples went back to the things [they’d left] behind and wouldn’t walk with him anymore.
So Jesus asked the twelve: ‘Don’t you want to go back also?’
But Simon Peter answered, ‘Lord, who should we go off to? You say the words of life in the age,
and we have known and believed that you’re the Holy One of God!’
Then Jesus said, ‘I chose you twelve, didn’t I? But one of you is a slanderer.’
Of course, he was talking about Judas ([the son] of Simon IsCariot), because; although he was one of the twelve, he was about to betray [Jesus].
After that, Jesus [limited] his travels just to Galilee, for he no longer cared to travel through Judea, because the Judeans were looking to kill him.
However, the Jewish Festival of Tents was coming up,
so his brothers said to him: ‘Leave here and go to Judea so your disciples there can also see the things you’re doing.
Because, no one does things in secret when he wants everyone to know what he’s saying.’
(It’s a fact that his brothers didn’t believe in him.)
So Jesus told them: ‘It isn’t my time yet… but it’s always your time.
For, the world doesn’t have any reason to hate you, but it hates me, because I testify to the fact that what it does is wicked.
So, you go on up to the festival… I’m not going to this festival, because my time hasn’t arrived yet.’
And he remained in Galilee [a while] after telling them this.
However, after his brothers went to the festival, he also went (not openly, but in secret).
The Judeans were looking for him at the festival, for they were asking, ‘Where is that [man]?’
And there was a lot of discussion about him among the crowds. Some would say, ‘He’s a good man.’ But others would say, ‘No he isn’t; He misleads the masses.’
Of course, nobody would talk about him publicly, because they were all afraid of the Judeans.
Then, when the festival was half over, Jesus finally [arrived and] went into the Temple and started teaching.
This caused the Judeans to wonder and ask, ‘How does this man understand the writings when he hasn’t studied them?’
So Jesus answered: ‘What I teach isn’t mine; it belongs to the One who sent me.
Anyone who wants to do His will knows whether the things I’m teaching come from God, or if I’m making this about by myself.
He who tells things that he made up about himself is trying to glorify himself. But, he who wishes to glorify the One who sent him is true, and there isn’t any unrighteousness in him.
‘Didn’t Moses give you the Law? Yet, none of you obey the Law. For, why are you looking to kill me?’
And the crowd answered, ‘You’re demonized! Who’s looking to kill you?’
So Jesus said, ‘I did just one thing that bothers you.
Moses gave you the circumcision (not that it’s from Moses but from your forefathers), thus you circumcise men on the Sabbath.
So, if a man is circumcised on a Sabbath to keep from breaking the Law of Moses, why are you furious with me because I made an entire man healthy on a Sabbath?
Quit judging from the way things look on the surface; Judge righteously!’
At that, some who lived in Jerusalem asked, ‘Isn’t this the man that they’re looking to kill?
Look… he’s here speaking in public, and they don’t say anything to him! Have the rulers come to know for a fact that he’s the Anointed One?
We all know where this man is from, but when the Anointed One comes, no one will know where he’s from!’
Well, as he was standing there teaching in the Temple, Jesus shouted aloud, ‘You know me and you know where I’m from! But coming here wasn’t my idea. He who sent me is real and you don’t know Him.
I know Him, because I came from Him, and because He sent me here!’
So they started looking for ways to grab him, but no one laid a hand on him, because his time hadn’t come yet.
However, many in the crowd started to believe him and said, ‘When the Anointed One arrives, he surely won’t perform more signs than this man has, will he?’
And the Pharisees overheard what the crowd was saying about him, so the Chief Priests and Pharisees sent officers to take him.
Then Jesus said: ‘I will be here with you just a little while longer, before I go to the One who sent me.
Then you will look for me but you won’t find me, and where I’m [going] you can’t come.’

So, the Judeans started asking each other, ‘Where does he plan to go that we won’t be able to find him? He doesn’t intend to go to those scattered among the Greeks and start teaching the Greeks, does he?
What does he mean when he says, You’ll look for me but won’t find me, and, Where I’m going you can’t come?’
Well, it was on the last day (the climax of the festival) that Jesus stood up and shouted, ‘If anyone is thirsty, let him come to me and drink!
For the scripture says this about those who put faith in me: Rivers of living water will flow from him.’

[Of course] when he said this, [he was talking] about the Breath [of God], which those who were putting faith in him were about to receive. But the Breath hadn’t arrived yet, because Jesus hadn’t been glorified yet.
Then some of the crowd who heard these words started saying, ‘This is surely the Prophet!’
while others were saying, ‘This is the Anointed One!’ But, some were saying, ‘The Anointed One won’t really come from Galilee, will he?
Didn’t the Scriptures say the Anointed One is from the line of David and from Bethlehem, the village that David came from?’
So the crowd became divided over him.
And although some among them [had come there] to grab him, nobody laid a hand on him.
And when the [Temple] officers returned to the Chief Priests and Pharisees, they were asked, ‘Why didn’t you bring him in?’
But the officers replied, ‘No man has ever spoken like this!’
Then the Pharisees asked, ‘You haven’t been misled also, have you?
Why, none of the rulers or Pharisees believe in him, do they?
But this crowd that doesn’t know the Law is cursed!’
Then NicoDemos (who had come to him previously and who was one of [Jesus’ followers]) said to them:
‘Our Law doesn’t allow us to judge a man unless we first hear from him and get to know what he’s up to, does it?’
And they answered, ‘You aren’t a Galilean too, are you? Search [the Scriptures] and you’ll see that no Prophet is to come from Galilee!’
So, Jesus spoke to them again, saying, ‘I am the light of the world, and whoever follows me won’t ever walk in darkness, but will have the light of life.’
Then the Pharisees said to him, ‘You’re testifying about yourself, so what you’re witnessing to isn’t true!’
And Jesus answered, ‘Even if I do testify about myself, my testimony is true, because I know where I came from and where I’ll return. But you don’t know where I came from or where I’m going,
because your judgment is fleshly. However, I don’t judge anyone;
and if I do judge, my judgment is true, because I’m not alone, for the Father who sent me is with me.
It’s written in your Law that whatever two men testify to is true;
so I’m testifying about myself, and the Father who sent me also testifies about me.’
Then they asked, ‘So, where is your Father?’ And Jesus answered, ‘You don’t know me or my Father, for if you knew me, you would also know my Father.’
And although [Jesus] said these things in the Treasury; while he was teaching in the Temple, nobody grabbed him, because his time hadn’t come.
He told them again: ‘I’m going away and you’ll look for me, but you will die in your sins; because, where I’m going you can’t come.’
So the Judeans started asking, ‘He’s not to kill himself, is he? Because he says, Where I’m going you can’t come.’
But he went on to tell them, ‘You come from the things below and I come out of things from above. You’re from this world, but I’m not from this world.
So, as I told you before; You will die in your sins… If you don’t believe that I’m [the one], you will die in your sins!’
Then they asked, ‘So, who are you?’ And Jesus replied, ‘Why am I even talking to you?
I have so many things to tell you and to judge about you, but the One who sent me is truthful, and I’m telling the world the things that I heard from Him.’
And because they really didn’t understand that he was talking to them about the Father,
Jesus said, ‘When you hang the Son of Man up, then you will know that I’m [the one] and that I’m not doing anything on my own; I’m just saying what that the Father taught me.
The One who sent me is with me, and He didn’t send me off alone, because I always do things to please Him.’
And as he was saying these things, many started believing in him.
Then Jesus addressed the Judeans who believed him: ‘If you hold onto these things that I’m saying, you’re really my disciples…
you will know the truth and the truth will set you free!’
But they replied, ‘We’re the seed of Abraham and we’ve never been slaves to anyone! So, why are you saying that we’ll be set free?’
Then Jesus answered, ‘I tell you the truth; Everyone who sins is a slave to sin,
and a slave doesn’t live in the house through the age. Only the son lives there through the age.
So, if the Son sets you free, you’ll really be free!
‘I know that you’re Abraham’s seed, but you’re looking to kill me, because what I’m saying isn’t getting through to you.
I’m telling you the things that I saw [while I was] with my Father, but you’re doing the things that you heard from your father.’
Then they said, ‘Well, our father is Abraham!’ And Jesus answered, ‘If you really are Abraham’s children, do what Abraham did!
Yet, you want to kill me… a man who told you the truth that I heard from God! Abraham wouldn’t do this,
so you’re doing the things that your father wants.’ Then they replied, ‘We weren’t fathered in some immoral relationship… we have one Father, God!’
And Jesus said to them, ‘If God were your Father, you would love me; because I came here from God. I didn’t come here on my own, He sent me!
‘[Do you know] why you don’t understand what I’m saying? Because you aren’t listening!

You are from your father the Slanderer and you want to do whatever your father wishes. Why, he was a murderer from the start… he didn’t stick with the truth, because truth isn’t in him. So when he tells a lie, he’s telling about something that’s his, because he’s a liar and the father of the lie.
However; because I’m telling the truth, you don’t believe me.
‘Who among you accuses me of sin? And if I’m telling the truth, why don’t you believe me?
He who comes from God listens to what God is saying… and that’s why you don’t listen; because you aren’t from God.’
Well at that, the Judeans said, ‘Aren’t we [telling the truth] when we say that you’re a demonized Samaritan?’
And Jesus answered, ‘I don’t have a demon; I’m honoring my Father and you’re dishonoring me.
I’m not trying to glorify myself, but there is One who is watching and judging.
I tell you the truth; If anyone obeys my words, he won’t see death in the age.’
Then the Judeans said to him, ‘Now we know that you’re demonized! Why, even Abraham and the Prophets died, but you say, If anyone obeys my words he won’t see death in the age!
You aren’t greater than our father Abraham or the Prophets who all died, are you? Just who do you claim to be?’
And Jesus answered, ‘If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing. But it’s my Father who glorifies me… the One who you say is your God,
but you never knew Him! I know Him, and if I should ever say that I don’t know Him, then I’d be like you, a liar. But I do know Him and I’m obeying His instructions.
‘Why, your father Abraham shouted in joy over the hope of seeing my day… he saw it and rejoiced!’
Then the Judeans asked, ‘You aren’t fifty years old yet, and still you’ve seen Abraham?’
And Jesus replied, ‘I tell you the truth; I existed before Abraham was born.’
And at that, they picked up rocks to throw them at him, but Jesus [went and] hid. Then he left the Temple.
Now, as he was walking along, he saw a man who had been blind since birth.
So his disciples asked, ‘Rabbi; Who sinned, this man or his parents, that [caused him to be] born blind?’
And Jesus replied, ‘It wasn’t because this man or his parents sinned. It happened so that the works of God could be demonstrated through him.
For, I must do the work of the One who sent me while it is still day, because the night is coming when no one can work.
As long as I’m in the world, I’m the world’s light.’
And after he said that, he spit on the ground, made a paste with the saliva, and put it on the man’s eyes.
Then he told him, ‘Go wash in the Pool of SiloAm’ (which translates as The Sent Place). So he went off and washed, and came back seeing.
Well, when his neighbors (who knew he used to be a beggar) saw this, they asked, ‘Isn’t this is the man who used to sit and beg?’
And others answered, ‘That’s him,’ while still others said, ‘No! It just looks like him.’ However, the man said, ‘I’m the one!’
Then they asked, ‘So, how were your eyes opened?’
And he answered: ‘The man called Jesus made a paste and smeared it on my eyes, then he told to me to go to SiloAm and wash. So I went and washed, and started seeing.’
Then they asked, ‘Where is that man?’ And he answered, ‘I don’t know.’
So then they took the man who used to be blind to the Pharisees.
Well, the day that Jesus made the paste and opened his eyes was a Sabbath.
So the Pharisees asked him again how he had gained his sight. And he answered, ‘He put a paste on my eyes; then I washed and I started seeing.’

So, some of the Pharisees said, ‘This man isn’t from God, because he doesn’t observe the Sabbath!’ While others asked, ‘If the man’s a sinner, then, how can he perform such signs?’ As the result, they were divided.
So they asked the blind man once more, ‘Since he opened your eyes, what do you have to say about him?’ And the man said, ‘He’s a Prophet!’
However, the Judeans didn’t believe that he had once been blind and started seeing, until they called his parents
and asked them, ‘Is this your son who you say was born blind? And if so, then how can he see now?’
And his parents replied, ‘We know that this is our son and that he was born blind.
But we don’t know how he started to see or who opened his eyes. Ask him… he’s old enough. He has to speak for himself!’
His parents said this, because they were afraid of the Judeans, since they had already agreed that if anyone would admit that [Jesus] is the Anointed One, they would be expelled from the synagogue.
And that’s why his parents said, ‘He’s old enough, ask him.’
So, they called the man who used to be blind a second time and said, ‘Glorify God! We know that man is a sinner!’
But he answered, ‘Whether he’s a sinner or not, I don’t know. The only thing I do know is that I was blind and now I can see.’
Then they asked, ‘What did he do to you… how did he open your eyes?’
And he replied, ‘I told you already, but you aren’t listening! Why do you want to hear it again? Do you also want to become one of his disciples?’
Well at this, they spoke insultingly to him, saying, ‘You’re one of that man’s disciples, but we are disciples of Moses.
We know that God spoke to Moses, but we don’t know where this man is from!’
And the man said, ‘It’s truly marvelous that you don’t know where he’s from, yet he opened my eyes.
We know that God doesn’t listen to sinners, but he listens to anyone who is God-fearing and does His Will.
Why, through the ages, no one has ever heard of someone that could open the eyes of a person who was born blind.
And if this man wasn’t from God, he couldn’t do anything.’
Then they replied, ‘You were born a complete sinner, and now you’re teaching us?’ And at that, they threw him out.
Well, Jesus heard about them throwing him out, and when he found him, he asked, ‘Are you starting to put faith in the Son of Man?’
Then the man answered, ‘Just who is he Lord, so that I can believe in him?’
And Jesus said, ‘You have seen him, for he who is speaking to you is that one.’
Then he said, ‘I do believe in him, Lord,’ and he bowed low.
And Jesus said, ‘This is the judgment that I brought into the world: Helping those who don’t see to see, and those who see to become blind.’
But when some of the Pharisees who were with him heard this, they asked, ‘We aren’t blind also, are we?’
And Jesus replied, ‘If you were blind you wouldn’t have any sin. But because you claim to see, your sin remains.’
‘I tell you the truth; All those who don’t enter the sheep pen through the gate, but climb in at some other place, are thieves and robbers;
but he who enters through the gate is the shepherd of those sheep.
This one is the gatekeeper who opens to them, and the sheep listen to his voice… he calls his sheep by their names and leads them out.
And when he has them all out, he leads them, and the sheep follow him, because they know his voice.
They won’t follow a stranger, but will run from him, because they don’t recognize the voices of strangers.’
Jesus had given them this illustration, but they didn’t understand what he meant.
Then Jesus again said, ‘I tell you the truth; I’m the gateway to the sheep,
but all those who come ahead of me are thieves and robbers, and the sheep won’t listen to them.
I’m the gateway, and whoever enters through me will be saved… he can come in or he can go out and find a place to graze.
The thief only enters to steal, kill, and destroy. But I came so they can have life and plenty of everything!
‘I am the good shepherd, and a good shepherd will give his life for the sheep.
A hired hand that isn’t a shepherd and doesn’t own the sheep will leave them and run away whenever he sees a wolf coming, so the wolf can grab and scatter them.
This is because he’s just a hired hand, and he doesn’t really care for the sheep.
I’m the fine shepherd, so I know my sheep and they know me,
just as the Father knows me and I know the Father. And I’ll give my life for the sheep.
‘But I have other sheep that aren’t in this pen, and I must also lead them and they will listen to my voice, and they will [all] become one flock under one shepherd.
‘This is why the Father loves me… because I will lay down my life so I can receive it back again.
Nobody is taking it from me… I’m giving it away! For I’m authorized to give it and I’m authorized to receive it again… this is the commandment that I got from my Father!’
Then once again, the Judeans became divided over these words.
Many were saying, ‘He’s demonized and crazy. Why do you listen to him?’
But others were saying, ‘These aren’t the words of a demonized man, for a demon can’t open blind peoples’ eyes can it?’
Well, at the time, the Festival of Dedication was going on in Jerusalem. It was winter,
and Jesus was walking in the Temple among the Columns of Solomon.
It was there that the Judeans gathered around him and asked, ‘How long are you going to keep our lives hanging? Just tell us if you’re the Anointed One!’
And Jesus answered, ‘I [already] told you and you don’t believe it! Why, the things that I’m doing in my Father’s Name testify about me,
but you don’t believe it, because [none of] you are my sheep.
My sheep listen to my voice… I know them and they follow me,
so I will give them life in the age… none will be lost in this age, nor will anyone take them from my hands!
My Father who gave them to me is greater than everyone, and nobody can take them out of the hands of the Father,
for the Father and I are one.’
Then once again, the Judeans picked up rocks to stone him.
And Jesus said: ‘I’ve shown you so many good things that I did, which came from the Father. So, for which of these good deeds are you ready to stone me?’
And the Judeans answered, ‘We aren’t stoning you for good deeds, but for blasphemy! Why, although you’re just a man, you’re making yourself a god!’
Then Jesus answered, ‘Isn’t it written in your Law, I say that you are gods?
And if He called those who were spoken against in God’s Word, gods (and you can’t void the Scriptures),
how can you tell me (one who was made holy and sent into the world by the Father) that I blaspheme because I say that I’m God’s Son?
If I’m not doing what my Father wants, then don’t believe me.
But if I am… even if you don’t believe me, believe in what I’m doing! Then you will come to know (and keep knowing) that the Father is in me and I am in the Father.’
So they tried to grab him again, but he slipped out of their reach.
Then he went off and crossed the Jordan [River] to the place where John had started his baptizing, and he stayed there.
Many people came to him there and said, ‘John didn’t perform a single sign, but everything John said about this man is true!’
And many believed in him there.
Now, there was a sick man, Lazarus of Bethany, from the village of Mary and her sister Martha.
This was the Mary who had greased the Lord with perfumed ointment and wiped his feet dry with her hair. And Her brother Lazarus was now very ill,
so his sisters sent word [to Jesus], saying, ‘Lord, {Look!} the one who you care for is sick!’
But when Jesus heard this, he said, ‘This sickness isn’t for death, it’s for the glory of God – so that the Son of God will be glorified by it!’
Now, Jesus loved Martha, her sister, and Lazarus.
However, when he heard that [Lazarus] was sick, he actually stayed where he was for two more days.
And after that, he told the disciples: ‘Let’s go to Judea again.’
But the disciples reminded him, ‘Rabbi, the Judeans just tried to stone you, so, are you going there again?’
And Jesus answered: ‘Aren’t there twelve hours of daylight? And if anyone walks in the daylight, he doesn’t bump into things, because he sees the light of this world.
But if someone walks around at night, he bumps into things, because there isn’t any light in him.’
Then after he said these things, he told them, ‘Our friend Lazarus has lain down and I’m going there to wake him up.’
So, the disciples said to him, ‘Lord, if he’s just lying down, he’ll survive.’
However, Jesus was talking about his death, while they thought he was talking about lying down to sleep.
Then Jesus told them plainly, ‘Lazarus has died!
And I’m happy for your sakes that I wasn’t there, so you can believe. Let’s go to him!’
Then Thomas (who was called the Twin) said to the other disciples, ‘Let’s go along also, so we can die with [Jesus].’
Well, when Jesus arrived, he found [that Lazarus] had already been in the tomb for four days.
Now, Bethany was about two miles from Jerusalem and
many of the Judeans had come to visit Martha and Mary to console them about their brother.
So, when Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went to meet him; but Mary stayed home.
Then Martha told Jesus, ‘Lord, if you had just been here, my brother wouldn’t have died.
And yet, I know now that whatever you ask God for, He’ll give you.’
And Jesus told her, ‘Your brother will stand again!’
Then Martha said, ‘I know that he’ll stand again in the resurrection on the Last Day.’
But Jesus said to her, ‘I am the resurrection and the life; so, whoever believes in me, even if he dies, will live.
And all the living that believes in me won’t remain dead through the age. Do you believe this?’
And she answered, ‘Yes Lord; I have believed that you’re the Anointed One, the Son of God, and the one who was to come into the world.’
Then after saying this, she went off to call her sister Mary, and told her secretly: ‘The Teacher is here and he’s asking for you.’
And when [Mary] heard this, she got up quickly and went to him.
Actually, Jesus hadn’t gone into the village yet, but was still in the place where Martha had met him.
So, when the Judeans (who came to the house to console her) saw Mary get up quickly and go out, they followed her, thinking that she was going to the tomb to cry there.
And when Mary got to the place where Jesus was and saw him, she fell at his feet, saying, ‘Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldn’t have died!’
And when Jesus saw her and all the Judeans who came with her crying, he groaned a deep sigh and was visibly upset.
Then he asked, ‘Where have you laid him?’ And they replied, ‘Lord, come and see.’
Then Jesus broke into tears,
and the Judean [women] said, ‘See how much he cared for him!’
But some of them asked, ‘Wasn’t this man who opened the eyes of the blind man able to keep him from dying?’
Then, after groaning deeply again, Jesus went to the tomb, which was actually a cave with a [large piece of] stone on top of it.
And Jesus said, ‘Lift the stone.’ But Martha (the dead man’s sister) said, ‘Lord, by now he must stink, because it has already been four days!’
However, Jesus said, ‘Didn’t I tell you that if you believed you would see the glory of God?’
So, they lifted the stone. Then Jesus raised his eyes toward heaven and said, ‘Father; Thank You for listening to me!
I know that You always hear me, but I said this because of the crowd that’s standing around; so they can believe that You sent me.’
And after he said that, he shouted loudly, ‘Lazarus, come on out!’
Then, the man who had been dead came out with his hands and feet still bound with wrappings, and his face wrapped in a cloth. And Jesus said, ‘Untie and unwrap him!’
So, many of the Judeans who came with Mary and saw what he had done started believing in him.
However, some went off to the Pharisees and told them about the things that Jesus did.
Then the Chief Priests and Pharisees called the Jewish Court together and asked, ‘What should we to do, for this man performs so many signs?
Why, if we let him go on doing this, everyone will believe in him and the Romans will come and take away our land and our people!’
Well, one of the group, CaiAphas (who was High Priest that year), said to them, ‘You don’t know anything,
and you haven’t figured it out yet… that it’s best for one man to die for the people, so the whole nation isn’t destroyed!’
However, he didn’t come up with this by himself. It was because he was High Priest that year, that he prophesied that Jesus was to die for the nation…
and not only for the nation, but so that he could also bring the scattered children of God together as one.
Well, from that day on, they were meeting and discussing ways to kill him,
so Jesus no longer traveled openly among the Judeans. He left there and traveled through the countryside near the desert, to a city called EphraIm, where he remained with his disciples.
Now, the Judean Passover was coming, and many people had left the countryside for Jerusalem before the Passover, in order to undergo ceremonial cleansing.
And [all the while], people were looking for Jesus, asking each other (as they stood around in the Temple), ‘What do you think? Won’t he come to the Festival at all…
for the Chief Priests and Pharisees have given orders that if anyone knows where he is, they should tell it so they can grab him.’
Well, six days before the Passover, Jesus arrived at BethAny (the place where he’d raised Lazarus from the dead),
and they prepared a supper for him there. And as Martha was serving, Lazarus was reclining at the table with the others.
Then Mary took a pound of perfumed ointment (genuine nard that was very expensive) and greased Jesus’ feet, wiping his feet dry with her hair; and this filled the house with the scent of the perfume.
But Judas IsCariot (one of his disciples who was about to betray him) asked:
‘Why wasn’t this ointment sold for three hundred silver coins and given to the poor?’
However, he didn’t say this because he was concerned about the poor, but because he was a thief… he kept the money box, and he used to [steal some of] the money that was put into it.
Then Jesus said, ‘Leave her alone, so she can keep this observance of the day of my burial.
You always have the poor with you, but you won’t always have me.’
Well, a large crowd of Judeans had learned that he was there; and they came, not just to see Jesus, but also to see Lazarus who he’d raised from the dead.
As the result, the Chief Priests then met in counsel to kill Lazarus also,
because many Judeans were going there and believing in Jesus due to him.
Then, the next day, a huge crowd of those who had come to the festival, when they heard that Jesus was coming into Jerusalem,
took palm-tree branches and went out to meet him, shouting, ‘[God] save the Praised One who comes in Jehovah’s Name… the King of Israel!’
And when Jesus found a young burro, he sat on it, just as it was written,
‘Don’t be afraid, O daughter of Zion. {Look!} Your king is coming, sitting on the colt of a burro.’
And although his disciples didn’t pay any particular attention to this at first; after Jesus was glorified, they remembered these things were written about him and that they did these things.
Also, the crowd that was with him when he had called Lazarus from the tomb and raised him from the dead [was there], testifying about him.
And many in the throngs [of people] came to meet him, because they’d heard that he had performed this sign.
So the Pharisees talked among themselves saying, ‘Look… we aren’t getting anywhere! See, the whole world is following him!’
Well, among those who came to worship at the Festival were some Greeks,
who went up to Philip (from BethSaida of Galilee) and said, ‘Lord; We want to see Jesus.’
So Philip went and told Andrew, then Andrew and Philip went and told Jesus.
But Jesus replied, ‘The time has come for the Son of Man to be glorified.
I tell you the truth; Unless a kernel of wheat falls to the ground and dies, it remains just a kernel. But if it dies, it produces much fruit.
So, he who is concerned about his life will lose it, but those who don’t care about life in this world will keep it safe, and they will live through the age.
‘If anyone wants to serve me, let him follow me, so that wherever I am, my servant will be also… for the Father will honor all who wish to serve me.
‘Now I am deeply disturbed, so what should I say… Father, save me from this hour? Why, this is why I came to this hour!
Father; Glorify Your Name!’ Then a voice came out of the sky, saying, ‘I glorified it and I will glorify it again!’
Well, [many in] the crowd who were standing there heard [the sound] and said it was thunder; but others said, ‘A messenger [from God] spoke to him!’
Then Jesus said: ‘This voice didn’t happen for me, but for you;
for now this world will be judged; now the ruler of this world will be thrown out;
and if I’m hung high off the ground, I will draw all men toward me.’
(He was really saying this to show what type of death he was about to die.)
Then the crowd said, ‘The Law tells us that the Anointed One will be here through the age. So, why are you saying that the Son of Man must be hung up… just who is this Son of Man?’

And Jesus replied, ‘The light will be among you just a little while longer; so walk while you have the light, so that darkness doesn’t overtake you. He who walks in darkness doesn’t know where he’s going.
But as long as you have the light, have faith in that light, so you can become Sons of Light.’ And after Jesus said these things, he went and hid from them.
For, although he had performed so many signs in front of them, they still didn’t believe in him.
This was so that the words of the Prophet IsaiJah were fulfilled that said, ‘O Lord; Who has believed in the things that we’ve heard, and who has shown the arm of Jehovah?’
The reason why they couldn’t believe, is as IsaiJah again said,
‘He has blinded their eyes and hardened their hearts, so they won’t see with their eyes, understand in their hearts, and turn around so I will heal them.’
IsaiJah said this, because he saw and talked about [Jesus’] glory.
Yet, even many of the rulers actually believed in him, but they wouldn’t admit it because of the Pharisees, and so they wouldn’t be expelled from the synagogue…
for they loved the glory of men even more than the glory of God.
Then Jesus shouted [to the crowd], ‘Those who believe in me don’t just believe in me alone, but in the One who sent me…
and whoever sees me, sees the One who sent me!
For, I came as a light to the world, so that everyone who believes in me won’t stay in darkness.
And if anyone hears what I say and doesn’t obey it, I won’t judge him; for I didn’t come to judge the world, but to save it!
Those who ignore me and don’t welcome what I say have one that judges them… the things that I’ve said are what will judge him on the last day,
